critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The only test files present are test_auth.py (15 unit tests for auth layer) and test_webhook.py (unit tests for webhook signature parsing). There are zero E2E or integration tests covering the build pipeline — no tests for session creation, generator execution, evaluator scoring, or the push phase. The smoke_test.py is a standalone HTTP script requiring a live server, not an automated pytest integration test.
critical.recommendation: Add a pytest integration test file (e.g., tests/test_pipeline_e2e.py) that mocks the Claude SDK and exercises the full session → evaluator → push flow without requiring a live server.

critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: scripts/smoke_test.py is a live-server HTTP probe script invoked manually or post-deploy; it is not wired into pytest and does not test the internal build pipeline logic (sessions, evaluator, push). The CLAUDE.md also documents 3 pre-existing failures in test_sdk_phase2.py due to claude_agent_sdk not being installed locally, meaning the SDK integration layer has no passing automated tests.
critical.recommendation: Promote smoke_test.py checks into a pytest-compatible test file using httpx or requests with a pytest-server fixture, and fix or explicitly skip the sdk_phase2 failures so CI reflects true coverage state.

high.reality: No explicit /start command handler is present in dashboard/app/api/telegram/route.ts. The file sets up conversation history, Linear helpers, and a triggerBuild() function, but the command-dispatch logic (the section that would match message.text against '/start') is not visible — the file is truncated before the POST handler body.
high.recommendation: Confirm the POST handler at the bottom of the file contains explicit branches for /start, /help, and /build; if absent, add them with appropriate responses (welcome message, help text, and build trigger respectively).

high.reality: No /help command handler is visible anywhere in the provided source. The bot appears to route all input through the Anthropic SDK as conversational turns (CEO-delegation pattern) rather than implementing discrete slash-command handlers.
high.recommendation: Add an explicit /help branch before the Anthropic fallback that returns a static list of supported commands so users can discover bot capabilities without relying on the LLM.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: No test file covers sessions.py, evaluator logic, or the push phase (_phase_push). The two existing test files (test_auth.py, test_webhook.py) cover only authentication and webhook signature verification — orthogonal subsystems with no pipeline involvement. Coverage of the core autonomous pipeline is effectively zero in the automated test suite.
high.recommendation: Create tests/test_sessions.py with mocked SDK responses to cover at minimum: session creation, phase transitions, evaluator score parsing, and push-phase branch naming, asserting correct state after each phase.

low.reality: triggerBuild() helper function exists and is partially visible, indicating /build intent is coded, but the actual text-matching branch that maps the Telegram message '/build <repo> <brief>' to this function is cut off and cannot be verified in the provided excerpt.
low.recommendation: Verify the POST handler contains a '/build' branch that parses repo URL and brief from the message text and calls triggerBuild(); add a usage hint reply when arguments are missing.
